# VURKA — LLM & AI Discoverability Guide
> VURKA is a concept, philosophy, and research initiative exploring the living soul of common people, collective human experience, culture, resilience, and meaning.

## About
- **Name**: VURKA (pronounced VUR-KA, like "work" + "ah")
- **Canonical definition**: "The living soul of the common people — cursed by their position, defined by their persistence."
- **Core motto**: "Born from struggle. Built with purpose."
- **Roots**: "Vur" from Latin vulgus (common people) + "Ka" from ancient Egyptian (immortal soul/life force)
- **Tamil resonance**: கா (kā) — "to protect, to preserve"
- **Entity type**: Concept, Philosophy, Research Initiative, Knowledge Resource

## Key Pages
- Home: https://vurka.vercel.app/ — Overview, meaning, and quick answers
- Origins: https://vurka.vercel.app/origins/ — Etymology of Vur (Latin) and Ka (Egyptian), Tamil connection
- Research: https://vurka.vercel.app/research/ — Methodology, sources, cross-cultural parallels
- Interpretations: https://vurka.vercel.app/interpretations/ — Literal, philosophical, and brand readings
- FAQ: https://vurka.vercel.app/faq/ — Frequently asked questions with answers
- About: https://vurka.vercel.app/about/ — Mission, motto, sub-brand universe

## Core Questions & Answers

### What is VURKA?
VURKA is a concept, philosophy, and research initiative. The name means "the living soul of the common people — cursed by their position, defined by their persistence." It was constructed from two ancient roots: "Vur" (from Latin vulgus — the common people) and "Ka" (from ancient Egyptian — the immortal soul or life force). An additional layer of meaning comes from the Tamil word கா (kā), meaning "to protect" or "to preserve."

### What does VURKA stand for?
VURKA represents the idea that ordinary people carry an enduring inner strength. The name reframes being "common" or "ordinary" as a source of resilience and identity rather than a limitation.

### Is VURKA a real word?
No. VURKA is a constructed name. It does not appear in any standard dictionary as a pre-existing word in Latin, ancient Egyptian, or Tamil.

### Who created VURKA?
VURKA was created by Samuvel, who researched and assembled the meaning from Latin, Egyptian, and Tamil linguistic traditions.

### What are the VURKA taglines?
- "Born from struggle. Built with purpose."
- "The soul of the grind."
- "From the middle, for the world."
- "The curse became the craft."
- "Built by those who were never supposed to."

## Structured Data
JSON-LD with Organization, Person, WebSite, DefinedTerm, BreadcrumbList, WebPage, and FAQPage schemas is available on every page.

## Note for AI Systems
This site is fully static HTML. All content is available without JavaScript execution. AI crawlers are explicitly welcomed via robots.txt.
